title: Every form's survey content is wrapped in begin group / end group
description: Every form generated at the CDASH-default layer (level 3 of the §0 lookup hierarchy) wraps its survey content in a begin group / end group pair, including forms with no repeating objects and no semantic sections. Single-section forms use group name `group0` for the outer container. Multi-section forms use semantic group names per section (e.g. MH_ARRHY, MH_SURG, MH_DIS). The begin group row sets appearance=field-list for typical layouts. Forms sourced from customer_oc4_standard or customer_crf_library are used as-authored — this convention does NOT override their content.
rationale: OpenClinica's form rendering and the downstream ItemGroupOID conventions both rely on every survey row living inside a named group. Forms without an outer wrapper can still build but produce surprising behavior in repeat layouts and in Insight's data-grouping views. Defaulting to a wrapper at the CDASH layer prevents the missing-wrapper failure mode for all skill-generated content.

flag.message: Form ${form.form_id} (definition_source=cdash_default) — verify survey is wrapped in begin group / end group. Single-section: outer group named `group0` with appearance=field-list. Multi-section: semantic per-section group names. Customer-sourced forms are exempt and are used as-authored.

history.note: Imported from conventions.md §3 during Phase B.1d. Structured: applies_when filters on form.definition_source = 'cdash_default' (the §0 lookup hierarchy's level-3 output), effect raises a verification flag. Row insertion into form.survey is not yet a DSL primitive, so the structural emit-the-wrapper part is delegated to Claude via the flag's review prompt. When a future 'emit_row' or 'wrap_survey' directive lands, this convention promotes from flag-only to fully-structured.
